content:
I'm trying to apply some patches from Alice, this is the macro that the patch changed:

macro_rules! static_lock_class {
    () => {{
        // SAFETY: The returned `LockClassKey` is stored in static memory and we pin it. Drop never
        // runs on a static global.
        static CLASS: $crate::sync::LockClassKey = unsafe {
            $crate::sync::LockClassKey::new_static()
        };
        $crate::prelude::Pin::static_ref(&CLASS)
    }};
}

Now rustfmt is unhappy, and it wants me to do

static CLASS: $crate::sync::LockClassKey =
    unsafe { $crate::sync::LockClassKey::new_static() };

instead. But then clippy is unhappy because it fails to find the safety comments for the unsafe block (which it could find with the previous formating):

warning: unsafe block missing a safety comment
   --> ../rust/kernel/sync.rs:121:13
    |
121 |             unsafe { $crate::sync::LockClassKey::new_static() };
    |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    |
   ::: ../rust/kernel/block/mq/gen_disk.rs:125:17
    |
125 |                 static_lock_class!().as_ptr(),
    |                 -------------------- in this macro invocation
    |
    = help: consider adding a safety comment on the preceding line
    = help: for further information visit https://rust-lang.github.io/rust-clippy/master/index.html#undocumented_unsafe_blocks
    = note: requested on the command line with `-W clippy::undocumented-unsafe-blocks`
    = note: this warning originates in the macro `static_lock_class` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)
rustfmt isn't actually unhappy, right? It's a macro
Well, it wants me to change, so it's not happy.
I guess you put the safety comment in the ugly position :woman_shrugging:
yeah.

macro_rules! static_lock_class {
    () => {{
        static CLASS: $crate::sync::LockClassKey =
            // SAFETY: The returned `LockClassKey` is stored in static memory and we pin it. Drop
            // never runs on a static global.
            unsafe { $crate::sync::LockClassKey::new_static() };
        $crate::prelude::Pin::static_ref(&CLASS)
    }};
}
now I'm unhappy
file a bug with clippy I guess
Yep

Boqun Feng said:

now I'm unhappy

Why? Moving the comment as close to the unsafe block makes sense?
Well, it’s now adding a comment between “=“ and the expression.

Would you normally do

let x =
    // SAFETY: ...
    unsafe { .. };

?

Sometimes I do put a comment in a middle of a statement, e.g.

MyStruct {
    // SAFETY: ...
    foo: unsafe { ... },
}

but I think putting it above the entire statement should be accepted too.
I think that yes, we may consider this a bug in clippy. I'll look into this in the next days.

Boqun Feng said:

Would you normally do

let x =
    // SAFETY: ...
    unsafe { .. };

?

Yes, I think this style makes sense in some cases, and I think it is also OK in this case. It is easy enough for the eyes to parse and it does move the comment closer to the code that it relates to.
I agree. I think that it shouldn't be too hard to extend the lint to also cover those cases.
I think that the formatting is not the sole reason for the lint, e.g. playground does not lint. The macro may have something to do with it. I'll investigate further.
Looks indeed macro-related. This does lint: https://play.rust-lang.org/?version=stable&mode=debug&edition=2024&gist=4991f16be9de6d4223b19d7407f1bf67
Yep. I think the reason is that the clippy check indiscriminantly ignores spans coming from macros.

Correction: It doesn't, but there is a check for expansions at some place. We have a test case against macro-expanded stuff, which works, but this has no binding, so there appears to be some interaction.

Andreas Hindborg said:

Boqun Feng said:

Would you normally do

let x =
    // SAFETY: ...
    unsafe { .. };

?

Yes, I think this style makes sense in some cases, and I think it is also OK in this case. It is easy enough for the eyes to parse and it does move the comment closer to the code that it relates to.

The annoying part is that this lint warning was solely introduced by rustfmt, and I could argue the previous format is also fine. And as Gary pointed out, the missing safety comment warning only triggers if this is in a macro, i.e. in normal code it's fine.

It's more of the consistency of the tooling rather than which style is more reasonable.

The weird thing here is that the code which is responsible for finding the // SAFETY comment in a macro call (namely the span_from_macro_expansion_has_safety_comment function in clippy_lints/src/undocumented_unsafe_blocks.rs, line 688) doesn't appear to pick this up.
We had similar issues in the past (outside macros, though), linked in our usual lists, i.e. I think this may be https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/13024.
